Based on the retrieved evidence, the correct definition of ICD-10 remains unchanged from our initial discussions—ICD-10 stands for **International Classification of Diseases, 10th revision**, representing a universal system used by healthcare professionals for diagnosing and coding illnesses and injuries. Each piece of evidence either supports or indirectly confirms this point without introducing conflicting information. For instance, references to legislative acts mentioning ICD-10 (like HIPAA compliance issues) indicate widespread acceptance and application of this nomenclature in regulatory contexts. There is no indication suggesting alternative meanings beyond those we initially discussed.
